text.title: k8s-resource-requests-limits Failure Modes
text.summary: Migrated antipatterns for k8s-resource-requests-limits as failure modes. Add detector + repair clauses where missing.

failure-mode.description: Setting identical ResourceQuota for all namespaces regardless of team size or workload type.
failure-mode.detector: TBD — observable signal that this antipattern is happening
failure-mode.repair: One-size-fits-all quotas either over-constrain small teams or under-constrain large ones. Quota should be proportional to team need with 15-20% headroom for burst.

failure-mode.detector: TBD — observable signal that this antipattern is happening
failure-mode.repair: The scheduler ignores limits; it uses only requests for placement. A pod with limits but no requests gets BestEffort QoS despite having a limit, and the scheduler cannot guarantee the pod lands on a node with sufficient resources.

failure-mode.description: Ignoring CPU throttling metrics after setting limits.
failure-mode.detector: TBD — observable signal that this antipattern is happening
failure-mode.repair: CPU throttling degrades latency silently — the pod stays Running but response times increase. Monitor container_cpu_cfs_throttled_seconds_total and alert at >10% throttled periods.
